title: Ch 9.1 — Try-Catch Basics: Potion Shop
description:
Practice writing try-catch blocks to handle bad input gracefully.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Background
──────────────────────────────────────────────

You work at the Bank of Evil's potion shop. Customers type in
potion names and prices, but their input isn't always valid.
Your job is to handle bad input without crashing the program.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Class: PotionShop
──────────────────────────────────────────────

Complete these three methods. Each one needs exactly one try-catch.

(1) double parsePrice(String input)
      • Try to parse input as a double and return it.
      • Catch NumberFormatException.
      • If parsing fails, print:
            "Invalid price: " + input
        and return -1.0.

(2) String getPotionName(String[] potions, int index)
      • Try to return potions[index].
      • Catch ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.
      • If the index is out of range, print:
            "No potion at index " + index
        and return "Unknown".

(3) int countLetters(String word)
      • Try to return word.length().
      • Catch NullPointerException.
      • If word is null, print:
            "Cannot count letters of null"
        and return 0.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
HINTS
──────────────────────────────────────────────
• Double.parseDouble("abc") throws NumberFormatException.
• Accessing array[5] on a 3-element array throws
  ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException.
• Calling null.length() throws NullPointerException.
• Each method has ONE try block and ONE catch block — that's it.
